title: Defender starfighter

The '''Defender starfighter''', or '''Republic Systems Defender''', was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] developed by the [[Republic Engineering Corporation]] as part of the [[Defender (ship-design program)|Defender]] design program. It was based on the [[ATL Interceptor]] used by the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].

==Characteristics==
The Defender starfighter was an improved version of [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation's]] [[ATL Interceptor]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> The Defender was 5.3 meters long and armed with three [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]: one in the nose of the craft, and one each on either side beneath the wing. To save space and weight, the standard Defender starfighter was not equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]].<ref name="TDC" />

[[File:Defender_starfighter_2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Defender starfighters in combat.]]

Although the Defender was slow by contemporary standards in starfighter design (at only two-thirds of the top speed of an [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] or [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptor]]), it was far faster than the aging Republic [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] and [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] that were usually relegated to planetary defense.<ref name="TDC" />

Its extendible [[S-foils/Legends|S-foil]] maneuvering struts could retract into the spaceframe for non-combat situations, but once an enemy had been engaged, the struts swung down and forward to give the Defender better positioning for maneuvering-jet bursts. While sudden maneuvers in an atmosphere actually risked tearing off the struts (due to the G-forces, the gravity of the planet itself, and atmospheric drag), in space, the Defender could spin around 180 degrees in mid-flight by firing one maneuvering jet forward and one backward.<ref name="TDC" />

==History==
The original ATL Interceptors were used by the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] to defend [[Trigon One]]<ref name="EIT">{{DroidsCite|Escape Into Terror}}</ref> in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].</ref> in addition to use in short-range [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] defense.<ref name="TEGtW" />

Based upon this design,<ref name="TEGtW" /> the Defender entered service with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] relatively quickly in the years after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="TBoC">{{GalaxyCite|2|The Battle of Cadinth}}</ref><ref name="TEGtW" />

[[File:DefTie.jpg|thumb|A Defender chasing a TIE Interceptor.]]
The New Republic Defender was intended to fulfill the need for a system space and atmospheric defense starfighter. Before the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] defeated the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], its fighter design philosophy emphasized fast ships that could evade Imperial pursuit by jumping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. However, when the New Republic gained control and the remnants of the Empire became disenfranchised, New Republic worlds became desperate for a fighter that could fend off Imperial raids.

The design was originally intended to deploy with the [[Nebula-class Star Destroyer|''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer]] and [[Defender-class assault carrier|''Defender''-class assault carrier]], but these ships were delayed for the best part of a decade,<ref name="TEGtW" /> and although the fighter saw service in campaigns like the liberation of [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol]]<ref name="TDC" /> and at the [[Battle of Cadinth]],<ref name="TBoC" /> it did not become as renowned as existing designs such as the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] or [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing]].

Eventually, some of these fighters found their way onto the black market—by [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], the smuggler [[Mazzic]] was using a pair of hyperdrive-equipped Defenders as gunships, and they impressed [[Talon Karrde]] so much, that he acquired two similarly-equipped ships of his own for use aboard ''[[Wild Karrde]]''. [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] similarly used a Defender one on her mission on [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. The craft she used however was not equipped with hyperdrive despite it also belonging to Karrde's organization. Jade's Defender launched from one of Karrde's ships the ''[[Starry Ice]]''.<ref name="SotP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DROIDSATLinterceptor.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ATL Interceptor from [[Kenner Products|Kenner's]] [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']] toy line.]]
The Defender starfighter was based on one of the last toys of the [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' toy line, the [http://www.toysrgus.com/index.php?action=disp_item&item_id=39639 ATL Interceptor] from the animated television series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']]. However, it had originally been designed as the [http://www.toysrgus.com/index.php?action=disp_item&item_id=49115 Death Star Defender], which was intended for use in the [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (toy line)|toy line]].<ref name="Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles">{{SW|url=news/offscreen-but-not-forgotten-mini-rigs-body-rigs-and-other-kenner-vehicles|text=Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles|int=Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles|nolive=1}}</ref>
